name: Diapason
tradition: Ancient Greek harmonic and monochord theory

name: Diapente
tradition: Ancient Greek harmonic and monochord theory
note: Perfect fifth in Greek interval nomenclature.

name: Diatessaron
tradition: Ancient Greek harmonic and monochord theory
note: Perfect fourth in Greek interval nomenclature.

name: Tonos
note: Major tone used as a structural step.

name: Apotome
note: Chromatic semitone complement to the limma.

name: Ditone
note: Two tones combined in Pythagorean tuning.

name: Chromatic Semitone
note: Common rational approximation used in modern reconstructions.

name: Chromatic Trihemitone
note: Larger chromatic-genus segment in many reconstructions.

name: Enharmonic Pyknon Lower
note: Lower pyknon part in some reconstructions; strongly context-dependent.

name: Enharmonic Pyknon Upper
note: Upper pyknon part in some reconstructions; strongly context-dependent.

name: Chromatic Pyknon Lower
note: Representative lower pyknon value for chromatic genus analyses.

name: Chromatic Pyknon Upper
note: Representative upper pyknon value for chromatic genus analyses.
